perturbation.original_text: 1.4 "Subject Invention" means any invention which is conceived or first ----------------- actually reduced to practice in the performance of work in connection with the Project.
perturbation.changed_text: 1.4 "Subject Invention" means any invention which is conceived or first ----------------- actually reduced to practice, regardless of the performance of work in connection with the Project.
perturbation.explanation: The original text ties the invention to the context of "the Project". The modified text removes this connection, potentially broadening the scope of "Subject Invention" to include any invention, even if unrelated. This may affect ownership rights defined in Article 8.1, particularly when considering rights to a particular Participant.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 8.1 Ownership of Subject Inventions. A Subject Invention shall be ------------------------------- considered a "Sole Subject Invention" of a Participant (i.e., solely owned by ---------------------- that Participant) if under the patent laws of the United States, inventorship resides solely in the employees, agents or contractors of that Participant. A Subject Invention shall be deemed to be a "Joint Subject Invention" (i.e., ----------------------- jointly owned in equal shares by the involved Participants) if under the patent laws of the United States, inventorship resides in one or more employees, agents or contractors of more than one Participant. Each Participant's ownership (if any) of a Subject Invention shall be subject to (a) the rights (if any) of the United States government in said Subject Invention pursuant to the Cooperative Agreement, and (b) the rights and licenses expressly granted under this Agreement or any separate agreement pertaining to the practical application of Subject Inventions and/or Project Works.
perturbation.justification: The original text defines 'Subject Invention' in connection with 'the Project'. The changed text removes this connection, broadening the definition to inventions regardless of their connection to the project. This directly contradicts Article 8.1, which defines ownership of 'Subject Inventions' by Participants. By removing the project connection, the change creates a contradiction about who owns inventions conceived outside the scope of the Project, causing uncertainty about which participant has the right to the invention as mentioned in Article 8.1.

perturbation.original_text: 3.3 Obligations of Terminated or Withdrawing Participant. The terminated ---------------------------------------------------- or withdrawing Participant will negotiate a transition plan with the Management Committee, and will work in good faith with such Management Committee with respect to transferring unfinished work with respect to the Project to the other Participants or a prospective new participant selected by the Management Committee in its sole discretion. Any costs associated with transferring unfinished work shall be borne by the terminated or withdrawing Participant. The terminated or withdrawing Participant hereby agrees that upon such termination or withdrawal the party or parties designated by the Management Committee to replace said Participant shall have a paid-up, nonexclusive, irrevocable license under said Participant's Project Intellectual -3- <PAGE> Property Rights to the extent (and only the extent) necessary to complete the tastes said Participant under this Agreement or the Cooperative Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: 3.3 Obligations of Terminated or Withdrawing Participant. The terminated ---------------------------------------------------- or withdrawing Participant will negotiate a transition plan with the Management Committee. Any costs associated with transferring unfinished work shall be borne by the Consortium. The terminated or withdrawing Participant hereby agrees that upon such termination or withdrawal the party or parties designated by the Management Committee to replace said Participant shall have a paid-up, nonexclusive, irrevocable license under said Participant's Project Intellectual -3- <PAGE> Property Rights to the extent (and only the extent) necessary to complete the tastes said Participant under this Agreement or the Cooperative Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: The original text says "Any costs associated with transferring unfinished work shall be borne by the terminated or withdrawing Participant". The change version says: "Any costs associated with transferring unfinished work shall be borne by the Consortium". It is clearly contradictory with the previous text and Article 9.1 Disclaimer of Joint and Several Liability, because one Participant is now responsible for the withdrawing party costs. This creates a contradiction about the payment obligation in case of one party withdraws or is terminated.
perturbation.location: Article 3 - Terminal, or Withdrawal of Participants, 3.3
perturbation.contradicted_text: 9.1 Disclaimer of Joint and Several Liability. Joint and several liability ----------------------------------------- shall not attach to the Participants; no Participant is responsible for the actions of any other Participant, but is only responsible for those tasks assigned to it in the SOW and for its own and its subcontractors' compliance with the terms and conditions of the Cooperative Agreement. Each Participant shall be responsible for any loss, cost, damage, claim or other charge to the extent arising out of or is caused by the actions of that Participant or its employees or agents or contractors. No Participant shall be liable for any loss, cost, damage, claim or other charge that arises out of or is caused by the actions of any other Participant or its employees or agents or contractors.
perturbation.justification: The changed text shifts the responsibility for costs associated with transferring unfinished work from the terminated/withdrawing participant to the Consortium. This directly contradicts Article 9.1, which states that no participant is responsible for the actions of any other participant and that each participant is responsible for their own costs. By making the Consortium responsible for the withdrawing participant's costs, the changed text introduces a contradiction regarding liability and financial obligations, as explained in the Perturbation Explanation. This makes the sections incompatible and creates a strong in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: 4.2 Authorizing With Respect to Cooperative Agreement. The Participants ------------------------------------------------- hereby authorize the Project Manager to negotiate with NIST on their behalf the terms and conditions of the Cooperative Agreement, subject to final review and written approval of such terms and conditions by each Participant prior to its execution. The Project Manager has provided to each Participant a copy of the Cooperative Agreement, attached hereto as Exhibit D. By executing this Collaboration Agreement, each Participant grants to the Project Manager a power of attorney which authorizes the Project Manager to execute the Cooperative Agreement attached as Exhibit D on behalf of that Participant and further agrees to be bound by the terms and conditions of the Cooperative Agreement as if such Cooperative Agreement had been executed by such Participant in its individual capacity.
perturbation.changed_text: 4.2 Authorizing With Respect to Cooperative Agreement. The Participants ------------------------------------------------- hereby authorize the Project Manager to negotiate with NIST on their behalf the terms and conditions of the Cooperative Agreement. By executing this Collaboration Agreement, each Participant grants to the Project Manager a power of attorney which authorizes the Project Manager to execute the Cooperative Agreement attached as Exhibit D on behalf of that Participant and further agrees to be bound by the terms and conditions of the Cooperative Agreement as if such Cooperative Agreement had been executed by such Participant in its individual capacity.
perturbation.explanation: The original text explicitly states that the Project Manager's negotiation with NIST is "subject to final review and written approval of such terms and conditions by each Participant prior to its execution." By removing this, the Project Manager can make commitment without the express acceptance and review of the participants.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 2.3 Relationship of the Participants. The Consortium is a contractual -------------------------------- relationship among the Participants, and is not an entity with separate legal existence. Nothing in this Agreement or the Cooperative Agreement shall constitute, create, give effect to, or imply, a joint venture, partnership, agency relationship, formal business organization or any type of permanent arrangement of any kind. Except as specifically provided herein, no Participant shall have the right to make commitments of any kind for or on behalf of any other Participant without the prior written consent of such other Participant.
perturbation.justification: The original text required participant approval before the Project Manager could finalize the Cooperative Agreement. The changed text removes this requirement. Section 2.3 states that no participant can make commitments on behalf of another without prior written consent. By removing the requirement for approval, the Project Manager can now potentially make commitments on behalf of participants without their consent, directly contradicting section 2.3.

perturbation.original_text: 9.1 Disclaimer of Joint and Several Liability. Joint and several liability ----------------------------------------- shall not attach to the Participants; no Participant is responsible for the actions of any other Participant, but is only responsible for those tasks assigned to it in the SOW and for its own and its subcontractors' compliance with the terms and conditions of the Cooperative Agreement. Each Participant shall be responsible for any loss, cost, damage, claim or other charge to the extent arising out of or is caused by the actions of that Participant or its employees or agents or contractors. No Participant shall be liable for any loss, cost, damage, claim or other charge that arises out of or is caused by the actions of any other Participant or its employees or agents or contractors.
perturbation.changed_text: 9.1 Disclaimer of Joint and Several Liability. Joint and several liability ----------------------------------------- shall not attach to the Participants; however, Participants are jointly and severally liable for any failure to comply with NIST requirements with appropriate cost breakdowns. Each Participant shall be responsible for any loss, cost, damage, claim or other charge to the extent arising out of or is caused by the actions of that Participant or its employees or agents or contractors. No Participant shall be liable for any loss, cost, damage, claim or other charge that arises out of or is caused by the actions of any other Participant or its employees or agents or contractors.
perturbation.explanation: The original text says "Joint and several liability shall not attach to the Participants." The added text introduces joint and several liability for failing to comply with NIST requirements, directly contradicting the original statement. This is introducing joint and several liability by the participant, and this contradicts the original intention that such liability would not attach to the Participants.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 9.1 Disclaimer of Joint and Several Liability. Joint and several liability ----------------------------------------- shall not attach to the Participants; however, Participants are jointly and severally liable for any failure to comply with NIST requirements with appropriate cost breakdowns. Each Participant shall be responsible for any loss, cost, damage, claim or other charge to the extent arising out of or is caused by the actions of that Participant or its employees or agents or contractors. No Participant shall be liable for any loss, cost, damage, claim or other charge that arises out of or is caused by the actions of any other Participant or its employees or agents or contractors.
perturbation.justification: The original text in Section 9.1 explicitly states that "Joint and several liability shall not attach to the Participants." The changed text modifies this same section to include "however, Participants are jointly and severally liable for any failure to comply with NIST requirements...", directly contradicting the initial statement. Since the contradicted text is the changed text, this is an in-text contradiction because Section 9.1 now both prohibits and allows joint and several liability for participants, creating a direct conflict.
